tasks.subject: [agent-tools-elevation] [P1] Add pulseTools to global agentContext config schema
tasks.description:
Add pulseTools boolean toggle to the agentContext section of UserConfigSchema in packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts.

Implementation Steps:

1. In packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts, modify the agentContext section from:

agentContext: z
  .object({
    relayTools: z.boolean().default(true),
    meshTools: z.boolean().default(true),
    adapterTools: z.boolean().default(true),
  })
  .default(() => ({ relayTools: true, meshTools: true, adapterTools: true })),

To:

agentContext: z
  .object({
    relayTools: z.boolean().default(true),
    meshTools: z.boolean().default(true),
    adapterTools: z.boolean().default(true),
    pulseTools: z.boolean().default(true),
  })
  .default(() => ({ relayTools: true, meshTools: true, adapterTools: true, pulseTools: true })),

2. Verify USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS still parses correctly (it uses UserConfigSchema.parse({ version: 1 }) so the new default will be picked up automatically).

3. If there are existing config manager tests, verify they still pass. The new field has a default so existing configs without pulseTools will get true.

Acceptance Criteria:
- pulseTools boolean with default true added to agentContext in UserConfigSchema
- USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS includes pulseTools: true in agentContext
- Existing configs without pulseTools parse successfully
- Type UserConfig includes agentContext.pulseTools
- All existing tests pass
tasks.activeForm: Adding pulseTools to global config schema

tasks.subject: [agent-tools-elevation] [P4] Add agent/directory toggle and AgentCombobox to CreateScheduleDialog
tasks.description:
Add a radio group to CreateScheduleDialog for choosing between agent-linked and directory-linked schedules, create the AgentCombobox component, and update ScheduleRow to display agent info. 1. CreateScheduleDialog Updates at apps/client/src/layers/features/pulse/ui/CreateScheduleDialog.tsx: Add state: const [scheduleTarget, setScheduleTarget] = useState<'agent' | 'directory'>( agents && agents.length > 0 ? 'agent' : 'directory' ); const [selectedAgentId, setSelectedAgentId] = useState(); Fetch agents: const { data: agents } = useRegisteredAgents(); Add radio group UI above CWD picker: setScheduleTarget(v as 'agent' | 'directory')}>
{scheduleTarget === 'agent' && } {scheduleTarget === 'directory' && } Update form submission: include agentId when agent target selected, cwd when directory target selected. Default: 'agent' if there are registered agents, 'directory' otherwise. 2. AgentCombobox Component at apps/client/src/layers/features/pulse/ui/AgentCombobox.tsx: Uses shadcn Command (combobox pattern): - Lists agents from useRegisteredAgents() - Each item shows: color dot, icon emoji, agent name, project path in muted text - Searchable by name or path - Follows same UX pattern as DirectoryPicker Props: agents (array), value (string | undefined), onValueChange ((value: string | undefined) => void) 3. ScheduleRow Updates at apps/client/src/layers/features/pulse/ui/ScheduleRow.tsx: When schedule.agentId is set and resolved: - Show agent color dot, icon, and name When schedule.agentId is set but agent not found (unregistered): - Show AlertCircle icon in destructive color with "Agent not found" text When no agentId: - Show FolderOpen icon with CWD path in mono font Tests: CreateScheduleDialog.test.tsx: - Shows radio group for agent vs directory - Agent picker appears when "Run for agent" selected - Directory picker appears when "Run in directory" selected - Submits agentId when agent target selected - Submits cwd when directory target selected ScheduleRow.test.tsx: - Shows agent name and color dot when agentId resolved - Shows warning when agentId set but agent not found - Shows folder icon and CWD path when no agentId Acceptance Criteria: - Radio group toggles between agent and directory target - AgentCombobox renders searchable list with color dots, icons, names, paths - Default target is 'agent' when registered agents exist - Form payload includes agentId or cwd based on selection - ScheduleRow displays agent info for agent-linked schedules - ScheduleRow shows warning for unresolved agent IDs - ScheduleRow shows folder icon and CWD for directory schedules - All component tests pass
tasks.activeForm: Adding agent picker to schedule creation and agent display to schedule rows

tasks.subject: [agent-tools-elevation] [P5] Update AGENTS.md and contributing guides with tool filtering documentation
tasks.description:
Update project documentation to reflect the new tool filtering system, context builder changes, and UI changes.

Files to Update:

1. AGENTS.md:
- Add tool-filter.ts to server services list: services/core/tool-filter.ts -- Per-agent MCP tool filtering. resolveToolConfig() merges agent manifest enabledToolGroups with global defaults. buildAllowedTools() produces the allowedTools list for SDK query(). Implicit grouping: binding tools follow adapter toggle, trace tools follow relay toggle.
- Update context-builder.ts description to mention agent-aware gating, peer agents block, pulse tools block
- Update CapabilitiesTab description to include tool group toggles
- Update AgentContextChips description to mention per-agent state
- Add pulseTools to agentContext description
- Update SettingsDialog tab count and add Tools tab description
- Update AgentDialog tab count (5 to 4)
- Add agentId to PulseSchedule description
- Add enabledToolGroups to AgentManifest description
- Add useAgentToolStatus to entities/agent hooks list
- Add AgentCombobox to features/pulse UI components list

2. contributing/architecture.md:
- Add Tool Filtering section to the agent-manager data flow area
- Include the per-session tool resolution data flow: sendMessage -> readManifest -> resolveToolConfig -> buildSystemPromptAppend(cwd, meshCore, toolConfig) -> buildAllowedTools -> query()

3. contributing/data-fetching.md:
- Add useAgentToolStatus as a hook pattern example showing how to combine multiple data sources in a derived hook

4. contributing/design-system.md:
- Document the 3-state chip pattern: enabled (normal), disabled-by-agent (opacity-40, [off] suffix), disabled-by-server (hidden)

Acceptance Criteria:
- AGENTS.md updated with all new modules, hooks, and UI changes
- architecture.md includes tool filtering data flow
- data-fetching.md includes useAgentToolStatus pattern
- design-system.md documents 3-state chip pattern
- All documentation accurately reflects the implemented code

tasks.subject: [agent-tools-elevation] [P5] Create ADRs for per-agent tool filtering and cascade disable
tasks.description:
Create Architecture Decision Records for the key architectural decisions in this feature. Check decisions/manifest.json for the actual next number before creating files.

ADR 1: Per-Agent Tool Filtering via allowedTools

Create decisions/00XX-per-agent-tool-filtering-via-allowedtools.md:

Frontmatter: number, title: Per-Agent Tool Filtering via allowedTools, status: accepted, created: 2026-03-04, spec: specs/agent-tools-elevation/02-specification.md

Context: Spec #88 introduced global config toggles for tool context injection. All agents shared the same tool configuration. The Claude Agent SDK supports an allowedTools option in query() that filters MCP tools at the SDK level.

Decision: Implement per-agent tool domain toggles stored in .dork/agent.json as enabledToolGroups: { pulse?, relay?, mesh?, adapter? }. Resolution merges agent overrides with global defaults, gated by server feature flags. Both MCP allowedTools filtering and context block omission are always in sync via resolveToolConfig() + buildAllowedTools().

Consequences: Each agent can have a tailored tool palette without affecting others. Defense-in-depth: disabled tools won't appear in MCP or context, but this is not a security boundary. Backward compatible: existing manifests without enabledToolGroups inherit global defaults. Implicit grouping (binding to adapter, trace to relay) reduces configuration surface.

ADR 2: Implicit Tool Group Hierarchy

Create decisions/00XX-implicit-tool-group-hierarchy.md:

Context: Binding tools are adapter-specific. Trace tools are relay-specific. Users should not need to separately toggle sub-groups that logically belong to a parent.

Decision: Binding tools follow the adapter toggle. Trace tools follow the relay toggle. Only four top-level toggles are exposed: pulse, relay, mesh, adapter.

Consequences: Simpler UX: 4 toggles instead of 6+. Less granular: cannot enable relay but disable traces (unlikely need). Easy to extend: add new sub-groups under existing parents.

ADR 3: Cascade Disable on Agent Unregister

Create decisions/00XX-cascade-disable-on-agent-unregister.md:

Context: When an agent is unregistered from Mesh, Pulse schedules linked to that agent would fail at run time because resolveEffectiveCwd cannot find the agent.

Decision: On agent unregister, automatically disable all linked Pulse schedules (enabled=0, status='paused'). The cascade is implemented via an onUnregister callback on MeshCore.

Consequences: No orphaned schedules running against stale agent references. Schedules are disabled, not deleted -- can be re-linked. UI shows warning on ScheduleRow. Single callback pattern on MeshCore is extensible.

Update decisions/manifest.json to include the new ADRs and increment nextNumber.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Three ADR files created with correct frontmatter
- decisions/manifest.json updated with new entries and incremented nextNumber
- ADRs follow Michael Nygard format (Context, Decision, Consequences)
- Status is accepted for all three
tasks.activeForm: Creating architecture decision records for key decisions
